title: Psychiatry referral documentation

red_flags.text: Supports documenting severe functional impairment, self-harm or suicide risk, substance misuse, self-neglect, treatment history, home and community support, family impact, referral, and collaborative care planning.
red_flags.rationale: Supports specialist assessment for high-risk GAD presentations for psychiatry referral documentation; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting severe functional impairment, self-harm or suicide risk, substance misuse, self-neglect, treatment history, home and community support, family impact, referral, and collaborative care planning.

source.title: Generalised anxiety disorder and panic disorder in adults: management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.37–1.2.39 — high risk and specialist assessment

referral.text: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, questions, safety planning, treatment or medication decisions, monitoring, follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment selection or doses.
referral.rationale: Supports clinician-led depression referral context for psychiatry referral documentation; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, questions, safety planning, treatment or medication decisions, monitoring, follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment selection or doses.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression

exact_section.heading: Sections 8–9 — clinician-led management, monitoring, and referral

referral.text: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.
referral.rationale: Supports psychosocial assessment and care context after self-harm for psychiatry referral documentation; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Reason, urgency, diagnosis, symptoms, mental state, risk, previous care, medicines, consent, destination, communication and safety plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for psychiatry referral documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
